# FreeToolDev

> Free bulk/batch utility tools for developers and designers. All tools run client-side in the browser — no signup, no file uploads to a server.

## Tools
- [Bulk Image Resize, Convert & Compress](https://freetooldev.com/tools/image-batch.html): Resize, convert, and compress dozens of images at once. PNG, JPG, WebP. Download individually or as a ZIP. Free, runs in your browser.
- [Bulk SVG Optimizer](https://freetooldev.com/tools/svg-optimizer.html): Strip editor metadata, comments, and excess coordinate precision from a whole batch of SVG files at once. Free, no signup, runs entirely in your browser.
- [CSV to JSON — Batch Converter](https://freetooldev.com/tools/csv-to-json.html): Convert multiple CSV files to JSON at once. Drag and drop, download individually or as a ZIP, completely free.
- [JSON ↔ YAML Converter — Free Batch Tool](https://freetooldev.com/tools/json-yaml-converter.html): Convert JSON to YAML or YAML to JSON, one document at a time or several separated by ---. Free, no signup, runs entirely in your browser.
- [CSV ↔ TSV Converter — Free Batch Tool](https://freetooldev.com/tools/csv-tsv-converter.html): Convert CSV to TSV or TSV to CSV with proper quote and delimiter handling. Paste text or upload multiple files, free, runs entirely in your browser.
- [Bulk JSON Validator & Formatter — Check Many Snippets at Once](https://freetooldev.com/tools/json-validator.html): Paste several JSON snippets at once — separated by a divider, no file upload — and validate and pretty-print every one in a single pass, with the exact line and column of any syntax error.
- [Base64 Encode / Decode — Free Online Tool](https://freetooldev.com/tools/base64.html): Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 back to text. Batch line-by-line processing supported, free, runs entirely in your browser.
- [Bulk URL Encoder / Decoder — Free Online Tool](https://freetooldev.com/tools/url-encoder.html): Percent-encode or decode a whole list of URLs, query strings, or parameters at once. Component and full-URL modes, free, runs entirely in your browser.
- [RSS Feed Generator](https://freetooldev.com/tools/rss-generator.html): Generate a valid RSS 2.0 feed from a simple list of titles, links, and dates. No account, no scraping — free and instant.
- [Sitemap.xml Generator](https://freetooldev.com/tools/sitemap-generator.html): Paste a list of URLs and generate a valid sitemap.xml instantly. Free, no server needed.
- [Bulk Sitemap Validator — Check Multiple sitemap.xml at Once](https://freetooldev.com/tools/sitemap-validator.html): Paste several sitemap.xml files at once and validate each against the Sitemap Protocol — malformed XML, invalid priority/changefreq/lastmod, duplicate URLs. Free, no signup.
- [robots.txt Generator](https://freetooldev.com/tools/robots-txt-generator.html): Generate a valid robots.txt in seconds — set disallowed paths, add your sitemap, and optionally block AI crawlers like GPTBot and ClaudeBot. Free, no signup.
- [llms.txt Generator](https://freetooldev.com/tools/llms-txt-generator.html): Turn a pasted list of your site's pages into a valid llms.txt file — a structured index that helps AI models like ChatGPT and Claude understand your site. Free, no signup.
- [Humans.txt Generator](https://freetooldev.com/tools/humans-txt-generator.html): List your team, tech stack, and a short note to generate a valid humans.txt credits file. No email or signup required, runs entirely in your browser.
- [Bulk Meta Title & Description Length Checker](https://freetooldev.com/tools/meta-length-checker.html): Paste several page titles and descriptions at once — no URL, no fetch — and check every one against safe length ranges in a single pass. Free, no signup.
- [Bulk Heading Structure Checker — Check H1-H6 on Many Pages](https://freetooldev.com/tools/heading-structure-checker.html): Paste the HTML of several pages at once and check H1-H6 heading hierarchy for each — missing H1s, skipped levels, duplicates, empty headings. Free, no signup.
- [IP / DNS / SSL Bulk Lookup](https://freetooldev.com/tools/ip-dns-ssl.html): See your own IP instantly, then look up IP addresses, DNS records (A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS), and SSL certificate expiry for a whole list of domains at once. Free, no signup.
- [Site Crawler & Audit — Auto-generate Sitemap, RSS & llms.txt](https://freetooldev.com/tools/site-crawler.html): Enter a URL and automatically crawl the site to generate sitemap.xml, rss.xml, and llms.txt, plus find broken links and missing meta tags — all in one pass.
- [JWT Decoder — Batch Decode Multiple Tokens](https://freetooldev.com/tools/jwt-decoder.html): Decode multiple JWTs at once, entirely in your browser. See header, payload, and expiry status for each token — nothing is sent to a server.
- [Bulk QR Code Generator — From a List of URLs](https://freetooldev.com/tools/qr-batch.html): Generate QR codes for a whole list of URLs or text at once. No upload, no account — runs entirely in your browser. Download individually or as a ZIP.
- [Bulk Barcode Generator — UPC, EAN, Code128](https://freetooldev.com/tools/barcode-batch.html): Generate UPC, EAN-13, or Code128 barcodes for a whole list of codes at once. No upload, no account — runs entirely in your browser. Download individually or as a ZIP.
- [Bulk Text Diff Checker](https://freetooldev.com/tools/text-diff-checker.html): Paste several original/modified text pairs at once and see a line-by-line diff for every one in a single pass. Free, no signup, runs entirely in your browser.

## Site
- [About](https://freetooldev.com/about.html)
- [Privacy Policy](https://freetooldev.com/privacy.html)
- [Contact](https://freetooldev.com/contact.html)

## Blog
- [Blog index](https://freetooldev.com/blog/index.html)
- [Line Diff vs Character Diff: Which One Actually Shows You What Changed?](https://freetooldev.com/blog/line-diff-vs-character-diff.html): Line-level and character-level diffing answer different questions. Here's when each one actually helps, and when finer detail just adds noise.
- [When You Actually Need to Diff Multiple Text Pairs at Once](https://freetooldev.com/blog/bulk-text-diff-use-cases.html): Comparing two versions of one file takes seconds anywhere. Here's when the job actually becomes a batch problem.
- [Meta Description vs Google's Auto-Generated Snippet: When Google Ignores What You Wrote](https://freetooldev.com/blog/meta-description-vs-google-snippet.html): Google rewrites a large share of meta descriptions with text pulled from the page instead. Here's what triggers a rewrite.
- [Why Your Meta Title Still Gets Cut Off Even Under 60 Characters](https://freetooldev.com/blog/meta-title-pixel-truncation.html): Google truncates titles by pixel width, not character count. A title under the "safe" limit can still get cut off.
- [robots.txt vs llms.txt vs humans.txt: Three Files, Three Audiences](https://freetooldev.com/blog/robots-llms-humans-txt-compared.html): Same location, same plain-text format, three completely different readers — a crawler, an AI model, and a curious human. Here's what each file actually controls.
- [What Is humans.txt? The File That's Not for Search Engines at All](https://freetooldev.com/blog/humans-txt-explained.html): No crawler reads it, no AI model parses it. Here's the format, the history, and whether it's worth adding anyway.
- [Why Your JSON Won't Parse: Every Common Syntax Error, Explained](https://freetooldev.com/blog/json-syntax-errors-explained.html): Trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys, comments — JSON rejects things JavaScript object literals allow. Here's why, with the exact fix for each.
- [Validating JSON Without Uploading It Anywhere](https://freetooldev.com/blog/json-validator-no-upload.html): Most online JSON tools are built around uploading files. When the JSON contains API tokens or real user data, that's a habit worth breaking — here's the actual difference.
- [Sitemap Index Files: When One sitemap.xml Isn't Enough](https://freetooldev.com/blog/sitemap-index-files.html): Every sitemap has a 50,000 URL limit. Here's how a sitemap index file works, when you actually need one, and how it's different from just having several sitemaps.
- [Sitemap Priority and Changefreq: Do They Actually Matter?](https://freetooldev.com/blog/sitemap-priority-changefreq.html): Two sitemap tags that developers spend real time tuning, and Google says it mostly ignores both. Here's what's actually true, and what's still worth getting right.
- [Multiple H1 Tags on One Page: Actual Problem or Old Advice?](https://freetooldev.com/blog/multiple-h1-tags.html): Google says it can handle multiple H1s fine. Most SEO guides still say use only one. Both are true at once — here's what's actually going on.
- [Why Skipping Heading Levels (H2 to H4) Actually Hurts You](https://freetooldev.com/blog/skipped-heading-levels.html): Jumping from H2 straight to H4 looks harmless on screen. Here's what it actually breaks for screen readers and search engines, and why it's an easy mistake to repeat everywhere.
- [Inline SVG vs <img> vs CSS Background: Choosing the Right One](https://freetooldev.com/blog/inline-svg-vs-img-vs-css-background.html): The same SVG file can be used three different ways on a page, and each one trades off styling control, caching, and accessibility differently. Here's how to pick.
- [Why Excel Mangles Your CSV Numbers (and How to Stop It)](https://freetooldev.com/blog/excel-csv-number-mangling.html): Leading zeros vanish, long IDs turn into scientific notation, dates rewrite themselves. None of this is a bug in the CSV file — it's Excel guessing, and you can turn the guessing off.
- [YAML Anchors and Aliases: Reusing a Value Without Repeating It](https://freetooldev.com/blog/yaml-anchors-aliases.html): Anchors and aliases let a YAML file define a block once and reuse it elsewhere, instead of copy-pasting the same config repeatedly. Here's how they actually work.
- [Double Encoding: How a URL Gets Encoded Twice by Accident](https://freetooldev.com/blog/double-url-encoding.html): A %20 that turns into %2520 is a sign a value got encoded twice somewhere in the pipeline. Here's how it happens and how to actually fix it.
- [What's Actually Bloating Your SVG Exports](https://freetooldev.com/blog/svg-export-bloat.html): An SVG exported from Illustrator or Figma is often 3-10x larger than it needs to be. Here's exactly what's taking up that extra space, and what's safe to remove.
- [JSON vs YAML: When Each One Actually Makes Sense](https://freetooldev.com/blog/json-vs-yaml.html): Both represent the same underlying data, so the choice usually comes down to who's reading and writing the file, not what the data looks like.
- [CSV vs TSV: Why the Delimiter Choice Isn't Just Cosmetic](https://freetooldev.com/blog/csv-vs-tsv.html): Comma and tab-delimited files look nearly identical in a spreadsheet, but the choice of delimiter changes how much quoting a file needs and how forgiving it is to hand-edit.
- [encodeURIComponent vs encodeURI: When to Use Which](https://freetooldev.com/blog/encodeuricomponent-vs-encodeuri.html): Two similarly-named functions that do genuinely different jobs. Mixing them up either breaks a URL or leaves a query value unsafe. Here's how to tell them apart.
- [When You Actually Need Barcodes in Bulk](https://freetooldev.com/blog/bulk-barcode-use-cases.html): Generating a single barcode takes seconds. Here's the actual point where it becomes a batch problem — inventory, product labeling, asset tags, and where it doesn't.
- [UPC vs EAN vs Code128: Which Barcode Format Do You Actually Need?](https://freetooldev.com/blog/upc-vs-ean-vs-code128.html): Three barcode formats cover almost every real-world case. Here's what each one is actually for, and how to tell which one your situation calls for.
- [robots.txt vs llms.txt: What AI Crawlers Actually Read](https://freetooldev.com/blog/robots-txt-vs-llms-txt.html): robots.txt controls whether a crawler can visit a page. llms.txt tells an AI model what that page actually is. They solve different problems and you need both.
- [robots.txt Mistakes That Accidentally De-Index a Whole Site](https://freetooldev.com/blog/robots-txt-mistakes.html): A handful of small robots.txt mistakes are common enough to have a name in SEO circles. Here's what they look like and how to avoid making them.
- [Static vs Dynamic QR Codes: What's the Real Difference?](https://freetooldev.com/blog/static-vs-dynamic-qr-codes.html): Dynamic QR codes are sold as the upgrade, but they solve one specific problem — and cost you something in return. Here's when you actually need one.
- [A Record vs CNAME vs TXT: DNS Records Explained](https://freetooldev.com/blog/dns-records-explained.html): A plain-language walkthrough of the DNS record types you'll actually run into — what each one does, and why the wrong one causes very specific, confusing failures.
- [CSV to JSON: Handling Data Types, Nesting, and Arrays Correctly](https://freetooldev.com/blog/csv-to-json-data-types.html): A CSV is flat text by design. Converting it to JSON forces a bunch of quiet decisions about types, nesting, and arrays that most converters get wrong by default.
- [XML Sitemap vs robots.txt: What Each One Actually Controls](https://freetooldev.com/blog/sitemap-vs-robots-txt.html): They live in the same root folder and get mentioned in the same breath, but a sitemap and robots.txt do almost opposite jobs. Here's what each one actually controls.
- [JWT Claims Explained: Registered, Public, and Private](https://freetooldev.com/blog/jwt-claims-explained.html): A plain-language guide to registered, public, and private JWT claims — iss, aud, sub, exp, jti, and the custom ones — and what each one is actually for.
- [How to Find Broken Links on Your Site Without Installing Anything](https://freetooldev.com/blog/find-broken-links-free-tool.html): A practical, step-by-step guide to finding broken links on a small site using a free browser-based crawler — no software install required.
- [RSS Generator Without an Account: Manual Builder vs. Auto-Scraper](https://freetooldev.com/blog/rss-generator-no-account.html): Most 'RSS generator' tools scrape someone else's site to build a feed. If you're building a feed for your own site, that's the wrong tool — here's the difference.
- [A Free, Browser-Based Alternative to Screaming Frog for Small Sites](https://freetooldev.com/blog/free-alternative-screaming-frog.html): Screaming Frog is the standard SEO crawler, but it's overkill (and eventually paid) for a small site. Here's when a lighter, free crawler covers what you actually need.
- [Using RSS to Feed Newsletters and Automation Tools](https://freetooldev.com/blog/rss-for-automation.html): RSS isn't just for feed readers anymore — it's the plumbing behind a lot of no-code automation. Here's how to put a feed to work with Zapier, Make, and newsletter tools.
- [When You Actually Need to Generate QR Codes in Bulk](https://freetooldev.com/blog/bulk-qr-code-use-cases.html): Making one QR code takes seconds anywhere. Here's when the job actually becomes a batch problem — restaurants, events, inventory, and more.
- [Tracking SSL Expiry Across Multiple Domains Without a Paid Service](https://freetooldev.com/blog/ssl-expiry-monitoring-free.html): Running a handful of sites doesn't require a paid SSL monitoring subscription. Here's a free approach to keeping track of certificate expiry across multiple domains.
- [Why Your CSV Turns to Gibberish When Converted to JSON](https://freetooldev.com/blog/csv-encoding-gibberish.html): Korean, Japanese, and other non-English CSV exports often turn to garbled characters when read as UTF-8. Here's what's actually happening and how to fix it.
- [Sitemaps for Static Sites: GitHub Pages, Netlify, and Vercel](https://freetooldev.com/blog/sitemap-static-sites.html): Static site generators don't always create a sitemap.xml for you. Here's how to generate and submit one, whichever host you're on.
- [Debugging JWTs and Base64 Tokens Without Sending Them to a Server](https://freetooldev.com/blog/debug-jwt-base64-locally.html): Pasting a live JWT or API token into a random online decoder is a bad habit. Here's how to decode Base64 and inspect tokens without your data leaving your browser.
- [WebP vs AVIF in 2026: Which Should You Batch-Convert To?](https://freetooldev.com/blog/webp-vs-avif-2026.html): A practical, no-nonsense comparison of WebP and AVIF for 2026 — browser support, compression, encoding cost, and which one to pick for a batch conversion job.
- [Why "No-Upload" Image Compression Is Safer for Client Work](https://freetooldev.com/blog/no-upload-image-compression.html): Uploading client images to a third-party server is a bigger risk than most people realize. Here's why browser-only, no-upload compression tools are becoming the standard for freelancers and agencies.
- [Converting 100 Images: AI or a Tool?](https://freetooldev.com/blog/batch-vs-ai-image-convert.html): A practical comparison of using AI chat tools versus a dedicated batch tool for converting, resizing, and compressing large numbers of images.
